maml:description.maml:para: Get Datto BCDR agents from a given device
The Get-DattoBCDRAgent cmdlet get agents from a defined BCDR device or for Endpoint Backup for PC agents (EB4PC)
To get agents from the Datto BCDR the SerialNumber of the BCDR needs to be defined
BCDR serial number to get agents from
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to get agents from
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets a list of Endpoint Backup for PC (EB4PC) clients and the agents under those clients
Returns the agents from the defined Datto BCDR
Returns the first 10 agents from Page 2 from the defined Datto BCDR

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Datto BCDR alerts for a given device
The Get-DattoBCDRAlert cmdlet gets Datto BCDR alerts for a given device
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber and returns any alerts
Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber with the first 10 alerts from the 2nd Page of results

maml:description.maml:para: Get Datto BCDR assets (agents and shares) for a given device
The Get-DattoBCDRAsset cmdlet gets Datto BCDR assets (agents and shares) for a given device
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber and returns any agents or shares
Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber and returns the first 10 agents or shares from the 2nd Page of results

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Datto BCDR devices from the the Datto API
The Get-DattoBCDRDevice cmdlet gets can get a one or more Datto BCDR devices
BCDR serial number to return data for
The parameter is mandatory if you want to get a specific device
Whether hidden devices should be included in your results
Acceptable values are: '0', '1'
By default '1' is returned by the API which shows hidden devices
Whether child reseller devices should be included in your results
Acceptable values are: '0', '1'
By default '1' is returned by the API which shows child reseller devices
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to return data for
The parameter is mandatory if you want to get a specific device
Whether hidden devices should be included in your results
Acceptable values are: '0', '1'
By default '1' is returned by the API which shows hidden devices
Whether child reseller devices should be included in your results
Acceptable values are: '0', '1'
By default '1' is returned by the API which shows child reseller devices
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets BCDR devices with any hidden or child reseller devices
Gets BCDR devices without any hidden or child reseller devices
Gets the first 10 Datto BCDR devices from the second Page Hidden and child reseller devices will be included

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Datto BCDR shares for a given device
The Get-DattoBCDRShare cmdlet gets Datto BCDR shares for a given device
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber and returns any shares
Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber with the first 10 shares from the 2nd Page of results

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Datto BCDR VM restores by serial number
The Get-DattoBCDRVMRestore cmdlet gets device VM restores by serial number
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
BCDR serial number to return data for
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gest Datto VM restores from the defined device
Gets the Datto BCDR VM restores with the defined SerialNumber with the first 10 restores from the 2nd Page of results

maml:description.maml:para: Gets an asset(s)(agent or share) for a specific volume on a device
The Get-DattoBCDRVolume cmdlet gets an asset(s)(agent or share) for a specific volume on a device
BCDR serial number to return data for
Volume name (id) of the protected volume
The content of the 'volume' field when calling /v1/bcdr/device/{SerialNumber}/asset
BCDR serial number to return data for
Volume name (id) of the protected volume
The content of the 'volume' field when calling /v1/bcdr/device/{SerialNumber}/asset

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto BCDR with the defined SerialNumber and returns any agents or shares for the defined volume

maml:description.maml:para: Get details for direct-to-cloud assets
The Get-DattoBCDRDTCAsset cmdlet get details for direct-to-cloud assets
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId
When defined with ClientID this filters the results returned from the /dtc/assets endpoint
When not defined with ClientID assets are returned from the /dtc/{clientId}/assets endpoint
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId
AssetUUID for the direct-to-cloud assets
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId
AssetUUID for the direct-to-cloud assets
When defined with ClientID this filters the results returned from the /dtc/assets endpoint
When not defined with ClientID assets are returned from the /dtc/{clientId}/assets endpoint
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets details for direct-to-cloud assets
Filters results for direct-to-cloud assets for a specific client
Gets details for direct-to-cloud assets for a specific client
Gets details for a specific direct-to-cloud asset

maml:description.maml:para: Get RMM templates
The Get-DattoBCDRDTCRMMTemplate cmdlet gets RMM templates for direct-to-cloud assets
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId
ClientID for the direct-to-cloud assets
Can be found in the organizationId

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets RMM templates for all direct-to-cloud assets
Gets RMM templates for the defined direct-to-cloud assets

maml:description.maml:para: Gets storage pool usage
The Get-DattoBCDRDTCStoragePool cmdlet gets storage pool usage for direct-to-cloud assets

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets storage pool usage for all direct-to-cloud assets

maml:description.maml:para: Updates the bandwidth settings for a single direct-to-cloud agent
The Set-DattoBCDRDTCBandwidth cmdlet updates the bandwidth settings for a single direct-to-cloud agent
UUID
Pause the agent while on a metered connection
Maximum bandwidth in bits for the agent
Maximum throttled bandwidth in bits for the agent
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
UUID
Pause the agent while on a metered connection
Maximum bandwidth in bits for the agent
Maximum throttled bandwidth in bits for the agent
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the bandwidth settings for the defined single direct-to-cloud agent

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the API public & secret keys used to authenticate API calls
The Add-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlet sets the API public & secret keys which are used to authenticate all API calls made to Datto
The Datto API keys are generated via the Datto portal Admin > Integrations
Defines your API public key
Defines your API secret key
Defines your API public key
Defines your API secret key

dev:remarks.maml:para: Prompts to enter in the API public key and secret key
The Datto API will use the string entered into the [ -ApiKeyPublic ] parameter as the public key & will then prompt to enter in the secret key
The Datto API will use the string entered as the secret key & will prompt to enter in the public key

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the base URI for the Datto API connection
The Add-DattoBCDRBaseURI cmdlet sets the base URI which is later used to construct the full URI for all API calls
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
Datto's URI connection point that can be one of the predefined data centers
The accepted values for this parameter are: [ US ] US = https://api.datto.com/v1
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
Datto's URI connection point that can be one of the predefined data centers
The accepted values for this parameter are: [ US ] US = https://api.datto.com/v1

dev:remarks.maml:para: The default base URI will use https://api.datto.com/v1
The default base URI will use https://api.datto.com/v1
A custom API gateway of http://myapi.gateway.celerium.org will be used for all API calls to Datto's API

maml:description.maml:para: Converts uri filter parameters
The Invoke-DattoBCDRRequest cmdlet converts & formats uri filter parameters from a function which are later used to make the full resource uri for an API call
This is an internal helper function the ties in directly with the Invoke-DattoBCDRRequest & any public functions that define parameters
Hashtable of values to combine a functions parameters with the ResourceUri parameter
This allows for the full uri query to occur
Hashtable of values to combine a functions parameters with the ResourceUri parameter
This allows for the full uri query to occur

dev:remarks.maml:para: Example: (From public function) $UriFilter = @{}
ForEach ( $Key in $PSBoundParameters.GetEnumerator() ){ if( $excludedParameters -contains $Key.Key ){$null} else{ $UriFilter += @{ $Key.Key = $Key.Value } } }
1x key = https://api.datto.com/v1/account?accountId=12345 2x key = https://api.datto.com/v1/account?accountId=12345&details=True

maml:description.maml:para: Exports the Datto BaseURI, API, & JSON configuration information to file
The Export-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet exports the Datto BaseURI, API, & JSON configuration information to file

Making use of PowerShell's System.Security.SecureString type, exporting module settings encrypts your API key in a format that can only be unencrypted with the your Windows account as this encryption is tied to your user principal This means that you cannot copy your configuration file to another computer or user account and expect it to work
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Validates that the BaseURI, API, and JSON depth are set then exports their values to the current user's Datto configuration file located at: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR\config.psd1
Validates that the BaseURI, API, and JSON depth are set then exports their values to the current user's Datto configuration file located at: C:\Celerium.DattoBCDR\MyConfig.psd1

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the Datto API public & secret key global variables
The Get-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlet gets the Datto API public & secret key global variables and returns them as an object
Decrypt and return the API key in plain text
Decrypt and return the API key in plain text

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto API public & secret key global variables and returns them as an object with the secret key as a SecureString
Gets the Datto API public & secret key global variables and returns them as an object with the secret key as plain text

maml:description.maml:para: Shows the Datto base URI global variable
The Get-DattoBCDRBaseURI cmdlet shows the Datto base URI global variable value

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows the Datto base URI global variable value

maml:description.maml:para: Gets various Api metadata values
The Get-DattoBCDRMetaData cmdlet gets various Api metadata values from an Invoke-WebRequest to assist in various troubleshooting scenarios such as rate-limiting
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
The default base URI is https://api.datto.com/v1
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
The default base URI is https://api.datto.com/v1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets various Api metadata values from an Invoke-WebRequest to assist in various troubleshooting scenarios such as rate-limiting
The default full base uri test path is: https://api.datto.com/v1
Gets various Api metadata values from an Invoke-WebRequest to assist in various troubleshooting scenarios such as rate-limiting
The full base uri test path in this example is: http://myapi.gateway.celerium.org/device

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the saved Datto configuration settings
The Get-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet gets the saved Datto configuration settings from the local system
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1
Opens the Datto configuration file
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1
Opens the Datto configuration file

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the contents of the configuration file that was created with the Export-DattoBCDRModuleSettings
The default location of the Datto configuration file is: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR\config.psd1
Opens the configuration file from the defined location in the default editor
The location of the Datto configuration file in this example is: C:\Celerium.DattoBCDR\MyConfig.psd1

maml:description.maml:para: Imports the Datto BaseURI, API, & JSON configuration information to the current session
The Import-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet imports the Datto BaseURI, API, & JSON configuration information stored in the Datto configuration file to the users current session
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1
Define the location to store the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is stored in the following location: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the name of the Datto configuration file
By default the configuration file is named: config.psd1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Validates that the configuration file created with the Export-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet exists then imports the stored data into the current users session
The default location of the Datto configuration file is: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR\config.psd1
Validates that the configuration file created with the Export-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet exists then imports the stored data into the current users session
The location of the Datto configuration file in this example is: C:\Celerium.DattoBCDR\MyConfig.psd1

maml:description.maml:para: Makes an API request
The Invoke-DattoBCDRRequest cmdlet invokes an API request to Datto API
This is an internal function that is used by all public functions
As of 2023-09 the Datto v1 API only supports GET and PUT requests
Defines the type of API method to use
Allowed values: 'GET', 'PUT'
Defines the resource uri (url) to use when creating the API call
Used with the internal function [ ConvertTo-DattoBCDRQueryString ] to combine a functions parameters with the ResourceUri parameter
This allows for the full uri query to occur
The full resource path is made with the following data $DattoBCDRModuleBaseUri + $ResourceUri + ConvertTo-DattoBCDRQueryString
Place holder parameter to use when other methods are supported by the Datto v1 API
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
Defines the type of API method to use
Allowed values: 'GET', 'PUT'
Defines the resource uri (url) to use when creating the API call
Used with the internal function [ ConvertTo-DattoBCDRQueryString ] to combine a functions parameters with the ResourceUri parameter
This allows for the full uri query to occur
The full resource path is made with the following data $DattoBCDRModuleBaseUri + $ResourceUri + ConvertTo-DattoBCDRQueryString
Place holder parameter to use when other methods are supported by the Datto v1 API
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Invoke a rest method against the defined resource using any of the provided parameters
Example: Name Value ---- ----- Method GET Uri https://api.datto.com/v1/account?accountId=12345&details=True Headers {Authorization = Bearer 123456789} Body

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the Datto API public & secret key global variables
The Remove-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlet removes the Datto API public & secret key global variables
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the Datto API public & secret key global variables

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the Datto base URI global variable
The Remove-DattoBCDRBaseURI cmdlet removes the Datto base URI global variable
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the Datto base URI global variable

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the stored Datto configuration folder
The Remove-DattoBCDRModuleSettings cmdlet removes the Datto folder and its files This cmdlet also has the option to remove sensitive Datto variables as well
By default configuration files are stored in the following location and will be removed: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define the location of the Datto configuration folder
By default the configuration folder is located at: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define if sensitive Datto variables should be removed as well
By default the variables are not removed
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Define the location of the Datto configuration folder
By default the configuration folder is located at: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Define if sensitive Datto variables should be removed as well
By default the variables are not removed
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Checks to see if the default configuration folder exists and removes it if it does
The default location of the Datto configuration folder is: $env:USERPROFILE\Celerium.DattoBCDR
Checks to see if the defined configuration folder exists and removes it if it does If sensitive Datto variables exist then they are removed as well
The location of the Datto configuration folder in this example is: C:\Celerium.DattoBCDR

maml:description.maml:para: Test the Datto API public & secret keys
The Test-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlet tests the base URI & API public & secret keys that were defined in the Add-DattoBCDRBaseURI & Add-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlets
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
The default base URI is https://api.datto.com/v1/bcdr
Define the base URI for the Datto API connection using Datto's URI or a custom URI
The default base URI is https://api.datto.com/v1/bcdr

dev:remarks.maml:para: Tests the base URI & API access token that was defined in the Add-DattoBCDRBaseURI & Add-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlets
The default full base uri test path is: https://api.datto.com/v1/bcdr/device
Tests the base URI & API access token that was defined in the Add-DattoBCDRBaseURI & Add-DattoBCDRAPIKey cmdlets
The full base uri test path in this example is: http://myapi.gateway.celerium.org/device

maml:description.maml:para: Gets a filtered list of activity logs ordered by date
The Get-DattoBCDRActivityLog cmdlet gets a filtered list of activity logs ordered by date
Defines a client name with which to do a partial/prefix match
Defines the number of days (unless overridden with sinceUnits), up until now, for which to produce logs
Defines the units to use for the since filter
Available values : days, hours, minutes
Defines a comma-separated array of targetType:targetId tuples
Example: bcdr-device:DC1234DC1234
Defines the type of target for which to find activity logs
Example: bcdr-device
Defines a username with which to do a partial/prefix match
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API
Defines a client name with which to do a partial/prefix match
Defines the number of days (unless overridden with sinceUnits), up until now, for which to produce logs
Defines the units to use for the since filter
Available values : days, hours, minutes
Defines a comma-separated array of targetType:targetId tuples
Example: bcdr-device:DC1234DC1234
Defines the type of target for which to find activity logs
Example: bcdr-device
Defines a username with which to do a partial/prefix match
Page number to return
Amount of items to return with each Page
Returns all items from an endpoint
This can be used in unison with -PerPage to limit the number of sequential requests to the API

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto BCDR platform activity logs from the past day
Gets the Datto BCDR platform activity logs from the past 7 day
Gets the Datto BCDR platform activity logs for the user named bob from the past 7 day
Gets the Datto BCDR platform activity logs from the defined targets for the past 30 day
Gets the Datto BCDR platform activity logs from the past 30 day
Returns the second Page of 10 items

maml:description.maml:para: Get Datto SaaS protection backup data for a given customer
The Get-DattoBCDRApplication cmdlet gets Datto SaaS protection backup data for a given customer
ID of the Datto SaaS organization
The number of days until the report should be generated
ID of the Datto SaaS organization
The number of days until the report should be generated

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto SaaS protection backup data from the define customer ID and does not include remote IDs
Gets the Datto SaaS protection backup data from the define customer ID and includes reports that will generated in the next 30 days

maml:description.maml:para: Gets detailed Saas backup data
The Get-DattoBCDRBackupStats cmdlet gets detailed Saas backup data for a given customer
ID of the Datto SaaS organization
ID of the Datto SaaS organization

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets detailed Saas backup data for the defined customer

maml:description.maml:para: Get Datto SaaS protection data about what domains are being protected
The Get-DattoBCDRDomain cmdlet gets SaaS protection data about what domains are being protected

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets SaaS protection data about what domains are being protected

maml:description.maml:para: Get Datto SaaS protection seats for a given customer
The Get-DattoBCDRSeat cmdlet gets Datto SaaS protection seats for a given customer
Defines the id of the Datto SaaS organization
Type of seat to return
Allowed values: 'User', 'Site', 'TeamSite', 'SharedMailbox', 'Team', 'SharedDrive'
Defines the id of the Datto SaaS organization
Type of seat to return
Allowed values: 'User', 'Site', 'TeamSite', 'SharedMailbox', 'Team', 'SharedDrive'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Datto SaaS protection seats from the define customer id

maml:description.maml:para: Sets Datto SaaS Protection bulk seat changes
The Set-DattoBCDRBulkSeatChange cmdlet is used to bulk set SaaS Protection seat changes
ID of the Datto SaaS organization
Defines the external Subscription ID used to set SaaS bulk seat changes
The ExternalSubscriptionId can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoBCDRApplication
Example: 'Classic:Office365:654321' 'Classic:GoogleApps:654321'
Defines the seat type to backup
This is a case-sensitive value
Seat Types can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoBCDRSeat
Example: Office365: 'User', 'SharedMailbox', 'Site', 'TeamSite', 'Team' Google: 'User', 'SharedDrive'
Defines what action to take against the seat
This is a case-sensitive value

Active (License): The seat exists in the organization and is actively backed up, meaning the seat is protected Paused (Pause): The seat exists in the organization; backups were enabled but are currently paused Unprotected (Unlicense): The seat exists in the organization but backups are not enabled
Allowed values: 'License', 'Pause', 'Unlicense'
Defines the comma separated target IDs to change
Remote IDs can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoApplication
Example: ab23-bdf234-1234-asdf
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
ID of the Datto SaaS organization
Defines the external Subscription ID used to set SaaS bulk seat changes
The ExternalSubscriptionId can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoBCDRApplication
Example: 'Classic:Office365:654321' 'Classic:GoogleApps:654321'
Defines the seat type to backup
This is a case-sensitive value
Seat Types can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoBCDRSeat
Example: Office365: 'User', 'SharedMailbox', 'Site', 'TeamSite', 'Team' Google: 'User', 'SharedDrive'
Defines what action to take against the seat
This is a case-sensitive value

Active (License): The seat exists in the organization and is actively backed up, meaning the seat is protected Paused (Pause): The seat exists in the organization; backups were enabled but are currently paused Unprotected (Unlicense): The seat exists in the organization but backups are not enabled
Allowed values: 'License', 'Pause', 'Unlicense'
Defines the comma separated target IDs to change
Remote IDs can be found by referencing the data returned from Get-DattoApplication
Example: ab23-bdf234-1234-asdf
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

maml:alert.maml:para: The bulkSeatChange API endpoint can be used for Seat Management 2.0 only. You cannot change seat status in Seat Management 1.0, but you can add SaaS Protection to seats as described in Exploring Seat Management features.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the Datto SaaS protection seats from the defined Office365 customer ID
Sets the Datto SaaS protection seats from the defined Google customer ID
